_comment: Editable copy. Team can tweak any string in here without touching engine code. Keys are referenced by data-copy= attributes in HTML and by shared.js.

brand.tagline_short: Three brands. One currency. One project at a time.
brand.tagline_long: South Africa's first Home + Life coalition rewards ecosystem.

landing.headline_html: A coalition loyalty programme built from the customer inwards, not the platform outwards.
landing.sub: A 10-minute interactive walkthrough of the HomeClub concept for Leroy Merlin SA, designed and prepared by the CMR Africa team.
landing.prepared_for_name: Naresh Rocharam, Treasury Manager · Leroy Merlin SA
landing.prepared_by: Prepared by the CMR Africa team · April 2026

pillars.blurb: Every tile, tap, tin of paint — earn HomeBucks as you build.
Coffee breaks, family meals, the rituals of the home.
Fitness, outdoor, the garden and the kids — all one currency.

landing.reframe_headline_html: Leroy Merlin already has a loyalty programme. HomeClub is the activation layer on top of it.
landing.reframe_body: my Home Card already captures identity and transactions at every till. 95% of that value sits dormant. HomeClub doesn't replace it — it turns a price-gate into a relationship, a coalition, and a source of live customer insight. Cheaper to build, faster to value, better internal story for the CEO.

disclosure.line: Status · Prototype for strategic discussion    ·    All data synthetic    ·    Coalition partners pending confirmation    ·    Prepared by CMR AFRICA for Leroy Merlin SA

steps.headline_html: You're already a my Home Card member.
steps.body: Since you downloaded the Leroy Merlin app and registered, you've been unlocking loyalty pricing at every till. Nothing changes — your my Home Card stays exactly as it is.

A new layer on top of my Home Card. The same identity, the same app, upgraded. You now earn HomeBucks on every rand you spend, start project windows that double your earn rate, and share points with Mugg & Bean and Decathlon.

steps.headline_html: You're in the NEST tier — free, and here's what's waiting.
steps.body: 1% back on everything. Monthly workshops. Birthday coffee at Mugg & Bean. Unlock HOME after your first completed project, and HOMESTEAD once you're all-in on the house.

admin_overview.headline: HomeClub · Pilot wave
admin_overview.sub: Live-view of the Leroy Merlin SA HomeClub pilot — 320 activated members across six stores

hero_kpi.label: my Home Card → HomeClub conversion
hero_kpi.caption_html: The number Naresh takes to the CEO. 320 of 438 pilot-targeted my Home Card members activated HomeClub within 14 days of invite.

insight_rotator.headline_html: Project-active members spend 3.4× more per week than non-project members.
insight_rotator.body_html: Across our 320-member pilot cohort, 134 members currently in an active 90-day project spent an average of R2,840 per week, versus R820 for members without an active project. The multiplier is largest in the Bathroom Renovation and Kitchen Upgrade categories.
insight_rotator.foot: Behavioural insight · weighted by store · panel n=320

insight_rotator.headline_html: Coalition cross-shoppers are four times more likely to hit HOME tier.
insight_rotator.body_html: Members who transacted at both Leroy Merlin and Mugg & Bean in the first 30 days reached HOME tier in 62 days on average, versus 248 days for single-brand shoppers. The coffee-break shopper is not a curio — it's the fastest tier-progression funnel in the model.
insight_rotator.foot: Tier progression velocity · pilot cohort · 30-day cross-shop window

insight_rotator.headline_html: Lapsed-member reactivation rate is 14% — with the right nudge.
insight_rotator.body_html: Members inactive for 60+ days responded to an unfinished-project nudge with a 500 HB booster at 14.3% — far above the 2–4% baseline for generic email campaigns. The trigger is the unfinished project, not the points. This is what no rewards platform can do on its own.
insight_rotator.foot: Reactivation test · n=112 lapsed members · 7-day response window

insight_rotator.headline_html: Eco-product buyers progress tiers 2.1× faster than matched non-eco buyers.
insight_rotator.body_html: Members who buy Water Warrior or Energy Warrior badge products (Solo Sanitaryware Eco Range, inverters, LED retrofits, water tanks) hit HOMESTEAD in 7.8 months, versus 16.4 months for matched control. Sustainability is a tier-progression accelerant, not a virtue signal.
insight_rotator.foot: Eco-purchase × tier correlation · n=128 eco-buyers · control matched on store + tenure

why_cmr.headline_html: We don't compete with the rewards platform. We sit upstream of it.
why_cmr.sub: TLC Worldwide Africa build the experience layer. Truth Loyalty build the strategy frameworks. CMR Africa builds the consumer insight layer that makes both of them work harder.

three.title: Pan-African consumer panel
three.body: A proprietary digital research panel reaching 140,000+ consumers across South Africa, Kenya, Nigeria, Ghana and Côte d'Ivoire. No other local partner can answer "what's happening in Cornubia vs. Fourways vs. Pretoria" with statistically valid segments, in a week.

three.title: Qualitative depth, not just dashboards
three.body: Our Bra Bens arm runs in-home ethnography, retail mystery shopping, and creative discovery workshops. The Weekly Insight panel in your dashboard isn't a chart — it's a human story, surfaced weekly by real researchers.

three.title: Creative delivery under one roof
three.body: Mystery-shop reports as visual storyboards, not 60-page Word docs. Campaign concepting baked into the insight. When the insight says "this segment wants water resilience first," we can build the Dulux-Jojo creative the same week.

why_cmr.compare_title: How we each address Leroy Merlin's brief
why_cmr.closing_title: The recommendation we're bringing Leroy Merlin
why_cmr.closing_body: Commission TLC (or whoever LM chooses) to run the rewards platform. Commission CMR Africa as the insight and strategy layer that feeds it the right campaigns, the right segments, and the right stories — week after week. Same brief, sharper answer.

mystery_shop.headline_html: A creative visual report, not a 60-page Word doc.
mystery_shop.sub: One of six store reports per quarter — Instagram-carousel format, designed to be forwarded without translation. Observations drawn from a live 90-minute shop at Fourways.

visit_meta.shopper: CMR Africa field researcher · Bra Bens
visit_meta.mode: Solo unaccompanied visit · fresh eyes protocol

overall.headline_html: Fourways feels like a destination, not a hardware run.
overall.summary: The single most retail-theatrical big-box DIY store in South Africa. Investment in ambient design (neon, festoon lighting, flower zone, Mugg & Bean destination) is far ahead of peers — but the operational layers on top (loyalty, wayfinding, digital, personalisation) have not caught up. HomeClub's job is to match the theatre with the data.

cards.obs: Neon "Electric Butterfly Flowers" + "#PetalPowered" + Edison bulb festoon + Pick me UP! cart. This is better retail theatre than most clothing stores on Sandton Drive.
cards.finding: Shoppers photograph this on the way in. Free earned media, zero gamification layer.
cards.homeclub: Add a photographable "Scan to start a project" neon cube at the flower zone exit. First project started in-store = 100 HB bonus + a Pick me UP! bloom.

cards.obs: "my Home Card — Want to join the club? More benefits to make your home the best place to live." Every red PROMO ticket carries "ALL PROMOTIONS ARE EXCLUSIVE TO LOYALTY MEMBERS."
cards.finding: Identity and transaction data is being captured at every till. 95%+ of the relational value is dormant.
cards.homeclub: HomeClub doesn't replace my Home Card — it activates it. Same card, same barcode, new behaviours on top.

cards.obs: Two colours in use: green "1st Price" (normal) + red strike-through (promo, loyalty-exclusive). Template is consistent, crisp, branded.
cards.finding: Visual system is strong and respected by ops. Room for a third colour without disruption.
cards.homeclub: Add coral "2× HomeBucks" ticket for items inside a member's active project window. Same template, orange band. See the In-store Pricing screen for the full mockup.

cards.obs: Bathroom and tile endcaps both carry "Need a contractor? Make the smart choice with Kandua" and "Standard installation fees available."
cards.finding: Co-brand is already live. No digital handoff — customer has to leave the aisle to book.
cards.homeclub: Wire Kandua into the app: scan endcap QR → match contractor in customer's suburb → 5% back as HomeBucks. Zero friction, shared revenue.

cards.obs: "Need to rent professional tools? We've got you covered with Coastal Hire" — clean co-branded endcap. No digital booking path visible in-store.
cards.finding: A second live partnership that's currently print-only. Natural HB redemption candidate.
cards.homeclub: One of the first HomeBucks redemptions we prototype: 800 HB = a tool day. Shows coalition working beyond coffee.

cards.obs: Full café (not a kiosk): ~40 seats, bakery counter, digital menu boards, muffin-wall mural, positioned near Exit. Real dwell-time zone.
cards.finding: Customers are already pairing shopping with a coffee. The coalition behaviour is live; the data isn't shared.
cards.homeclub: Coalition unlock moment #1. Shared identity + shared currency turns a coincidence into a coalition.

cards.obs: In-store paid service, tiered pricing (10–20 / 20–30 / 30–50 / 50+ pieces). Dedicated counter with Austro edge-banding machinery.
cards.finding: Premium service with no loyalty mechanic layered over it. HOMESTEAD members should be paying for this with HB.
cards.homeclub: A 10-piece cut-and-edge bundle at 400 HB. Demonstrates HB earn-and-burn on services, not SKUs.

cards.obs: Beautifully typeset black-and-white hanging signs (28 Moving boxes, 30 Glue/Tape, 61 Carpentry, 63 Exterior Doors). Entirely static. No screens. No QR. No loops.
cards.finding: Biggest single gap between LM's ambition and LM's execution. The store is analogue above the shelf.
cards.homeclub: QR-sticker every aisle sign. "Scan this aisle for today's bonus" becomes the default treasure hunt mechanic. 50–150 HB per challenge.

cards.obs: Water tanks displayed outside entrance. Solo Sanitaryware Eco Range (3.5L / 5L flush), Maldives water-saving toilets, Naterial artificial grass, Luxens paints — all visible.
cards.finding: Dufourq's three missions are already in the store. No connective storytelling above the shelf.
cards.homeclub: Water Warrior and Energy Warrior badges turn these SKUs into a journey. Eco-buyers progress tiers 2.1× faster in our model.

cards.obs: April BOGO campaign: "Fix your 'I'll fix it later' this April" — playful, confident copy. Matches LM's retail-theatre tone.
cards.finding: Copy voice is confident and specific. HomeClub must match, not flatten.
cards.homeclub: HomeClub copy system inherits the playful voice: "Your weekend called, it wants a project" on push notifications; "Coffee-while-you-shop" on coalition bundles.

mystery_shop.shopper_quote: Fourways spends like a retailer that wants to be more than a hardware store. But the moment I leave the entrance I'm back in 2010 — numbers hanging from the ceiling, paper tickets on shelves, and no one app-nudging me toward my project. HomeClub is the layer that makes the ambition legible.
mystery_shop.closing: Fourways is not a brief for a new loyalty programme. It's a brief for a connective layer over a store that already has 80% of what it needs.

in_store.sub: Leroy Merlin already runs a two-colour price ticket system — green for normal pricing, red for promotions. HomeClub adds a third colour — coral — for items that earn 2× HomeBucks during a member's active project window. Same template, same barcode, same ops flow.

tickets.label: 1st Price
tickets.note: Standard everyday price. No fine print.

tickets.label: Promo
tickets.note: ALL PROMOTIONS ARE EXCLUSIVE TO LOYALTY MEMBERS — existing standard fine-print line.

tickets.label: 2× HomeBucks
tickets.who: HomeClub members in an active project window
tickets.note: EARNS 2× DURING YOUR ACTIVE PROJECT WINDOW — HomeClub addition. Same promo mechanic, layered earn.

principles.title: Zero ops disruption
principles.body: Coral uses the identical template as green and red — same dimensions, same barcode placement, same POS integration. If ops can print a red ticket, they can print a coral one.

principles.title: Additive, not replacement
principles.body: Coral doesn't replace red. A promotional item inside a project window carries a coral ticket (promo + 2× earn stacked); outside a project window, the same item carries its red ticket (promo only). One SKU, one or two tickets depending on the season.

principles.title: Visible only where it matters
principles.body: Coral tickets only go on ~4-6% of SKUs per category — the ones anchored to the top project categories (paint, bath, garden, kitchen). This keeps the signal-to-noise high and the operational load light.

in_store.aisle_title_html: Turn the aisle sign into a scan point.
in_store.aisle_body: Fourways has beautifully typeset hanging aisle signs (28 Moving boxes, 30 Glue/Tape, 61 Carpentry, 63 Exterior Doors). They're entirely static. One QR sticker per sign — printed and affixed in a weekend — turns every aisle into a gamification surface.

aisle_ideas.title: Scan this aisle
aisle_ideas.body: 50–150 HB per completed aisle challenge. Refreshes weekly.

aisle_ideas.title: Treasure hunt mode
aisle_ideas.body: Find all 5 marked aisles in one visit → 500 HB + M&B coffee.

aisle_ideas.title: Category of the week
aisle_ideas.body: Whole category (e.g. Paint) runs 1.5× earn for all HomeClub members for 7 days.

aisle_ideas.title: Share the before
aisle_ideas.body: Post a before-shot of your project zone → 100 HB + creator tag.
